key_insight: The paper proposes Digi Turbine, a synthetic benchmark that combines a physics-informed neural network with Bayesian inverse identification and reliability screening to enable fast monitoring of offshore wind turbine monopile support structures.
engineering_relevance: It targets online structural health monitoring where high-fidelity finite element or aeroelastic simulations are too slow, offering a surrogate approach for state estimation from sparse measurements.
method: Physics-informed neural network trained with the Euler–Bernoulli beam equation on a Winkler soil foundation.
Bayesian-prior-informed inverse identification to estimate structural states/parameters.
First Order Reliability Method (FORM) used to screen reliability under uncertainty.
evidence_signal: Evaluation is entirely synthetic, using analytical or finite-difference ground truth configurations motivated by the NREL 5MW turbine context.
watch_item: Because validation is synthetic rather than field-tested, real offshore deployment may face model-form error, sensor noise, and soil-structure mismatch challenges.
paper_title: A Synthetic Reliability-Aware PINN Benchmark for Offshore Wind Turbine Support-Structure Monitoring with Bayesian Inverse Identification
